title: Cynanchum martinicense
intro: Cynanchum martinicense, also known as Martinique swallowwort, is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family, characterized by its fragrant flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cynanchum martinicense include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Cynanchum martinicense is commonly prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is also used in traditional remedies such as decoctions and salves to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Cynanchum martinicense is classified within the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family is part of the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants. The order Gentianales is a part of the clade asterids in the larger clade angiosperms. Angiosperms are a type of seed plant that produces flowers. The classification of Cynanchum martinicense reflects its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.
